title: campanula glomerata subsp. fatrae
intro: Campanula glomerata subsp. fatrae is a subspecies of the Campanula glomerata species, commonly known as the clustered bellflower, which is a perennial flowering plant native to Central Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Campanula glomerata subsp. fatrae include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
